oai_dc:dc.dc:title: Conservation News - Extinct Species
oai_dc:dc.dc:creator: Aviculture, American Federation of
oai_dc:dc.dc:description: This item originally appeared in AFA Watchbird Volume 48 Number 1 (October 2021).The following is an excerpt from the full PDF link.The Ivory Billed Woodpecker declared ExtinctCourtesy US Fish and Wildlife ServiceThe Fish and Wildlife Service announced Wednesday, September 29, 2021, that, based on a review of the best available scientific and commercial information, it has declared extinct 23 US species that were once listed under the Endangered Species Act and they propose to remove these species from the Federal Lists of Endangered and Threatened Wildlife and Plants. Comments from the public on these decisions are due by November 29 through the Federal eRulemaking Portal ( http://www.regulations.gov) or by letter received by that date. See https://www.federalregister.gov/documents/2021/09/30/2021-21219/endangered-andthreatened-wildlife-and-plants-removal-of-23-extinct-species-from-the-lists-ofClick the PDF link associated with this item to view the complete item as originally published.
oai_dc:dc.dc:source: AFA Watchbird; Vol. 48 No. 1 (2021): Watchbird October 2021; 22-23
